hypothesis: If a hybrid model that encodes surfaces with a 2D CNN and polymers with a GRU and fuses them via multi-head attention is trained on high-throughput MD-derived binding free energies with symmetry-aware augmentation, then it will predict binding free energies for unseen polymer–surface pairs with significantly lower MAE than baseline DNNs and alternative polymer encoders because attention captures sequence–surface interaction motifs and augmentation enforces rotational/flip invariances.
